questions.prompt: A module caller must choose an instance size. Which Terraform construct belongs at the module boundary?

options.feedback: Locals are internal expressions and cannot be supplied by callers.
Input variables define values callers can provide.
Outputs expose values from a module; they do not accept input.

feedback.why: Variables are the input side of the module interface.
feedback.common_misconception: Confusing locals with variables because both name values.

questions.prompt: Explain Terraform variables, locals, and outputs using a function analogy.
questions.answer: Variables are parameters, locals are helper variables inside the function, and outputs are return values.

feedback.why: The analogy maps module interface boundaries to function inputs and returns.

questions.prompt: Describe when to use variables, locals, and outputs in a Terraform module.
questions.answer: Use variables for caller-provided inputs, locals for internal named expressions, and outputs for values exposed to callers.

rubric.criterion: Identifies outputs as exposed return values

feedback.why: The distinction is about module interface direction and scope.
feedback.common_misconception: Using outputs as inputs or locals as external configuration.

questions.prompt: Classify each Terraform construct by its module-interface role.
questions.answer: Variables are inputs; locals are internal helpers; outputs are exposed returns.

items.text: Names a repeated expression inside the module
Exposes a value to parent modules

feedback.why: Each construct has a distinct scope and direction across the module boundary.
feedback.common_misconception: Thinking all named values are interchangeable.
